channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump says he wants to negotiate about Ukraine. It's not clear if Putin really does
item.description: Nearly three years after President Vladimir Putin invaded Ukraine, his troops are making steady progress on the battlefield. Kyiv is grappling with shortages of men and weapons. And the new U.S. president could soon halt Ukraine's massive supply of military aid.
item.dc:creator: EMMA BURROWS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Judge blocks Trump from placing thousands of USAID workers on leave and giving them 30-day deadline
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday dealt President Donald Trump and billionaire ally Elon Musk their first big setback in their dismantling of the U.S. Agency for International Development, ordering a temporary halt to plans to pull thousands of agency staffers off the job. U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Passport applications got more complicated for transgender Americans under new Trump policy
item.description: The day after President Donald Trump returned to office, Lisa Suhay took her 21-year-old daughter, Mellow, to a passport office in Norfolk, Virginia, where they live. Getting a passport for Mellow, who is transgender, was urgent.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's DEI order leaves academic researchers fearful of political influence over grants
item.description: BERKELEY, Calif. (AP) — President Donald Trump’s crackdown on diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives in programs receiving federal money has thrown into doubt the future of research Kendra Dahmer has been doing on intestinal parasites in India and Benin.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, TERRY CHEA and MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump says he's firing Kennedy Center board of trustees members and naming himself chairman
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Friday that he is firing members of the board of trustees for the Kennedy Center and naming himself chairman.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: House Divided: Wife an Eagles fan, husband cheers for Chiefs in NOLA
item.description: When one of the couple grows up in Kansas a Chiefs fan, and the other in South Jersey an Eagles fan, one would expect the marriage to be tested but Jaime and Dave Dailey make it fun.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic AGs sue over DOGE access to Treasury payment systems
item.description: “As the richest man in the world, Elon Musk is not used to being told ‘no,’ but in our country, no one is above the law,” James said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KRON4

item.title: Intoxicated Virginia man allegedly breaks into Bible study session thinking it was human trafficking operation
item.description: A Virginia man was arrested after breaking into a Bible study session he thought was a human trafficking operations, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump signs order to establish White House Faith Office
item.description: Trump directed the office to identify ways to reduce burdens on the free exercise of religion and direct all federal agencies to designate a “faith liaison” within 90 days.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Billabong, Quiksilver, Volcom to close all US locations
item.description: Liberated Brands, based in Costa Mesa, California, was the operator and licensee of Billabong, Quiksilver and Volcom in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mystery seed packages return to Texas
item.description: The state’s Department of Agriculture is warning residents not to open the mystery packages and report the items immediately.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, KRON4

item.title: Latino group calls for boycott of Coca-Cola, Walmart and more over deportations
item.description: This is part of an effort by a group called Freeze Latino Movement to boycott American companies while poking fun of the ongoing deportations launched by the Trump Administration and Immigration and Customs Enforcement, ICE.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Claims about USAID funding are spreading online. Many are not based on facts
item.description: As the Trump administration moves to dismantle the U.S. Agency for International Development, false and misleading information is being pushed on social media to support the change, much of it spread by the administration and Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: BY MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A mother and daughter were killed in tornadoes that struck eastern Tennessee
item.description: WARTBURG, Tenn. (AP) — Two tornadoes moved through eastern Tennessee, leaving a mother and daughter dead and injuring three other people, officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Musk says he'll bring back DOGE staffer under fire for racist posts
item.description: Elon Musk said Friday he will rehire a former Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) staffer who resigned after several of the aide's racist social media posts were exposed. "He will be brought back," Musk posted on X, the social media platform he purchased in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, NewsNation

item.title: Video shows Frontier passenger tackled after allegedly banging window mid-flight
item.description: An unruly passenger on a Frontier Airlines flight was tackled by fellow passengers Tuesday after they say he began punching an aircraft window.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Samsung S25 Ultra is the best Galaxy yet
item.description: Ever since I got my hands on the Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra a few weeks ago, I haven't stopped using it. I test out a lot of phones each year, and this is a sign that this is the best Galaxy smartphone Samsung has ever made.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: GOP-led House panel to hold hearing on USAID
item.description: The GOP-led House Foreign Affairs Committee will hold a hearing on the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) on Feb. 13, as the Trump administration moved to effectively shut down the agency and institute a mass foreign aid freeze. Committee Chair Brian Mast (R-Fla.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: DOGE staffer who accessed Treasury Department payment systems resigns after racist posts exposed
item.description: “Normalize Indian hate,” the account associated with Marko Elez posted in September, regarding people of Indian ethnicity who work in the U.S. tech sector, the Wall Street Journal reported.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, KRON4

item.title: Bond set at $20M for man accused in shooting at Ohio warehouse that killed 2 and wounded 4
item.description: NEW ALBANY, Ohio (AP) — A warehouse worker said in a frantic 911 call that he was shot in the neck and then took the gun from a co-worker after he had shot several others. Two people died and four were injured in the Ohio shooting that sent panicked employees fleeing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ohtani's former interpreter receives nearly 5-year sentence for stealing from star athlete
item.description: Ippei Mizuhara, who became a national figure for his on-field service and off-field friendship to Los Angeles Dodgers superstar Shohei Ohtani, has been sentenced to 57 months in prison for stealing millions from the Japanese phenom.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Chris Swanson, prominent sheriff and Democrat, announces run for Michigan governor
item.description: FLINT, Mich. (AP) — Chris Swanson, the high-profile sheriff of Michigan's Genesee County, is launching a Democratic bid to become the battleground state's next governor.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Arrest made in home invasion where men dressed as Amazon workers
item.description: The Savannah Police Department (SPD) has made an arrest in the home invasion that sent one man to the hospital in early January.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Austin ISD policy prevents parents from seeing video of alleged crimes against kids
item.description: He said his first-grade teacher trapped him behind a classroom door at Margaret T. Reilly Elementary School and then pushed on it with his weight.
item.dc:creator: Kelly Wiley, NewsNation

item.title: Sports reporter dies while in New Orleans for Super Bowl LIX
item.description: Adan Manzano died on Wednesday in New Orleans while on assignment to cover the Super Bowl for both Telemundo Kansas City and Tico Sports.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Mueller, KRON4

item.title: Cracker Barrel calls out Waffle House over egg surcharge
item.description: Cracker Barrel looks to be wooing Waffle House’s breakfast crowd with a new deal for loyal customers. The company announced Thursday that members of its Cracker Barrel Rewards program who order egg dishes through Wednesday, Feb.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Second type of bird flu found in US dairy cows
item.description: Experts said it raises new questions about wider spread and the difficulty of controlling infections in animals and the people who work closely with them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump attends bipartisan National Prayer Breakfast, a decadeslong Washington tradition
item.description: President Donald Trump participated in the National Prayer Breakfast at the Capitol on Thursday, joining a more than 70-year-old Washington tradition that brings together a bipartisan group of lawmakers for fellowship.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A dozen West Point cadet clubs ordered to disband, all others paused
item.description: The U.S. Military Academy at West Point, New York, has ordered a dozen cadet extracurricular and social clubs to immediately and permanently "cease all activities," with all affected groups centered on gender, race and ethnicity, the school acknowledged Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: 5 things to watch as the federal buyout deadline approaches
item.description: As the deadline approaches, agencies have amped up the pressure on employees, sending frequent “reminders” and a copy of the contract they would have to sign to accept the deferred resignation.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KRON4

item.title: Protesters in cities across the US rally against Trump's policies, Project 2025 and Elon Musk
item.description: Demonstrators gathered in cities across the U.S. on Wednesday to protest the Trump administration's early actions, decrying everything from the president's immigration crackdown to his rollback of transgender rights and a proposal to forcibly transfer Palestinians from the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: White House defends Trump's Gaza takeover, resettlement proposal
item.description: The White House on Wednesday defended President Trump’s proposal for the United States to take control of the Gaza Strip amid blowback from Arab leaders and some Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bondi sworn in as attorney general in Oval Office
item.description: Pam Bondi was sworn in as attorney general on Wednesday in the Oval Office with President Trump looking on and telling reporters she would be “as impartial as a person can be." “I know I'm supposed to say, ‘She's going to be totally impartial with respect to Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Armed teen arrested at Arkansas airport after allegedly demanding plane
item.description: After the employees got to safety, the suspect "breached through a door onto the airfield," an airport official said.
item.dc:creator: Hunter Trombetta, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Baltimore mayor fights back against attacks on DEI initiatives with ‘Definitely Earned It’ campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's attack on diversity, equity and inclusion programs have led military and government agencies — along with some private companies — to roll back observances of diversity and celebrations of Black History Month, which goes through February.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A timeline of last week's air disaster in Washington, the deadliest in the US since 2001
item.description: It has been nearly a week since a commercial jetliner and an Army helicopter collided in midair near Washington’s Ronald Reagan National Airport, killing 67 people in the deadliest U.S. air disaster since 2001.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 arrested in connection to string of high-end burglaries, including one at Joe Burrow's home
item.description: The men have been accused of selling the stolen goods pilfered from "wealthier neighborhoods" and, according to sources, the home of Cincinnati Bengals quarterback Joe Burrow.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, Fox 5
